phases.name: Bootstrap
phases.goal: Stand up the PhaseKeep repo for autonomous execution: Node ESM scaffold with a real node:test suite, cz_preflight wired to the npm scripts, VISION/ARCHITECTURE seeded from the proposal, everything committed on main.

exit_criteria.text: Node project scaffolded: package.json with ESM + engines >=24, `npm test` runs a node:test suite with at least one real test, `npm run build` (or lint/typecheck stand-in) exits 0
cz_preflight runs green with the tests/build gates wired to the npm scripts (no unwired-gate hints for tests/build)
docs/VISION.md and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md seeded from the proposal — cz_onboard no longer lists them as scaffold placeholders
Initial commits on main: (1) clauderized scaffold + proposal, (2) gameplan portfolio + seeded memory; working tree clean afterward

phases.name: Coverage Audit
phases.goal: Map every Phase-1 view data need (proposal §8.1: portfolio/gameplans, open items, invariants/decisions, graph+cascade, lessons/corrections, docs, reconciliation strip) to the installed clauderizer v1.11.0 JSON/ops surface; produce a committed gap matrix; file every gap as an open item; resolve the CLI-vs-MCP transport question (§16.4).

exit_criteria.text: docs/contract/COVERAGE.md committed, mapping every proposal 8.1 view (portfolio/gameplans incl. campaign detail, open items, invariants/decisions incl. approvals, graph+cascade+consumes, lessons/corrections+gauge, docs, reconciliation strip) plus exit-criteria state and baseline to concrete verbs/fields of the installed surface
Every gap (data reachable only via digest prose, cz_get on known IDs, or not at all) has an O-NN open item naming the missing listing verb or field
Schema-version and revision-signal presence/absence in current outputs verified and documented with captured output
clauderize doctor exit-code contract (0/1/2/3) and ops --list/--schema introspection audited as consumed contract surfaces alongside the read verbs
Raw capture set committed under docs/contract/captures/ as audit evidence
CLI-vs-MCP transport question (16.4) resolved as a recorded decision citing audit evidence

Portfolio reconciliation pass recorded: m1-m4 gameplans reviewed against the audit findings with amendments or explicit no-change verdicts per gameplan - they are provisional until this pass (D-001), and m1 Phase 0 does not start before it

phases.name: Engine Asks
phases.goal: Implement the audited contract gaps in /HOME/Clauderizer under that repo's own memory (D-003): monotonic status revision signal, explicit schema version field, missing listing verbs, and the assignment field on phases/gameplans (deciding its shape, proposal 16.7); cut a release and install it here.

exit_criteria.text: Installed clauderize --version reports a release newer than 1.11.0 (real release consumed via pip/uvx, not a dev checkout)
clauderize status --json carries an explicit schema version field and a monotonic revision that bumps on any engine write - verified by a scripted write-then-read against a scratch repo

The revision read path is near-free and MEASURED (O-03): a dedicated fast verb or blessed single-value contract artifact; with 10 projects polled at 1s intervals, steady-state poller CPU stays under 2% of one core on the Tier-1 box and p95 single-poll latency under 50ms native / 250ms through wsl.exe interop - numbers recorded via cz_add_output, revisable only with measurements attached
Every coverage-audit gap has its listing verb/field present in ops --list and reachable via CLI and MCP both
Assignment field on phases and gameplans is readable and writable through the ops registry as a minimal provisional shape (16.7), explicitly marked revisitable when m3 builds its first consumer
All engine-ask open items in this gameplan resolved; engine-side work tracked in the Clauderizer repo's own memory per D-003

phases.name: Fixture Corpus and Contract Client
phases.goal: Capture real JSON outputs from live clauderized repos into a committed fixture corpus, build the pure contract-client module (spawn, parse, validate, degrade) against fixtures alone, and land the same corpus in the engine repo's CI (proposal 15.5).

exit_criteria.text: Committed fixture corpus covers status, gameplans, focus, and every listing verb, including malformed/truncated/mid-write cases and a schema-version-mismatch case, captured from real repos of each kind (driven, loop, campaign)
Contract client module passes its full fixture suite offline (engine uninstalled or PATH-hidden): spawn abstraction, parse, validate, view models
Spawn abstraction owns the acquisition ladder (clauderize on PATH, then uvx --from clauderizer) and the minimum-version gate; both paths tested
Schema-version mismatch produces the explicit degrade message naming emitted version, supported version, and fix - tested
The same corpus runs in the Clauderizer repo's CI so contract breaks fail engine CI before release (15.5)
npm test green; baseline test count recorded via cz_add_output; Milestone 0 success met: UI developable against fixtures alone

phases.name: Server Core and State Reads
phases.goal: Build the tab-mode server core: project roots via args plus config, clauderize --json reads with the uvx fallback, per-project snapshot cache, revision-polling primary with same-side fs.watch and WSL boundary detection, last-known-good resilience, and the engine-missing degrade message.

exit_criteria.text: Tab-mode server starts on 127.0.0.1, accepts project roots via args plus config file, serves per-project state snapshots built from clauderize --json spawns with the uvx fallback form
Change detection per D-004: revision-signal polling primary; fs.watch on docs/ only for same-side roots with WSL cross-boundary detection choosing stat-polling, active mode surfaced per project
Malformed or partial reads keep the last-known-good snapshot with a staleness flag - fixture-tested, never a crash or blank
Missing or too-old engine yields the named degrade message with the fix (clauderizer >= x.y required, found z) - tested with PATH manipulation

The core's transport seam is proven by a second implementation: an in-process/test transport drives the same core API as the WS path, keeping D-006's transport-agnostic claim honest before Electron exists
A state change made by an agent in a real repo is visible in the pushed snapshot within 2s (watch) / 3s (poll)

phases.name: Security Baseline
phases.goal: Land the full section-11 control set before any further surface grows: token lifecycle, WS handshake auth, Host/Origin validation, CSP, sanitized markdown (D-007).

exit_criteria.text: Per-start crypto-random token >=128 bits; tokenized URL printed once; frontend stores it in sessionStorage and strips it via history.replaceState; subsequent HTTP carries it in a header
WS handshake authenticates via Sec-WebSocket-Protocol or authenticated first frame before any channel is serviced; unauthenticated connections closed silently
Every HTTP request validates Host and every WS upgrade validates Origin against 127.0.0.1:<port>; failures dropped before any handler
CSP prohibits inline script and restricts connect-src to own origin; markdown renders sanitized with no raw-HTML passthrough
Automated tests cover missing token, wrong token, foreign Origin, forged Host (rebinding shape), and confirm no bytes flow pre-auth

phases.name: Frontend Foundation and Brand
phases.goal: Preact publish-time-built static frontend with WS client, channel envelope, reconnect, command palette, cache-first sub-second paint; and the original PhaseKeep brand identity (proposal 7.3) recorded in docs/BRANDING.md with assets.

exit_criteria.text: Preact bundle built at publish time and served as static assets; npx startup performs no compilation
WS client with reconnect/backoff speaks the channel envelope ({ch: state | pty:<id>}) shared with future PTY channels
Command palette core interaction works: project switch, view navigation, gameplan jump
Dashboard paints from cached snapshot in under 1 second from open, then revalidates with the staleness flag covering the gap - measured

PhaseKeep brand identity (palette, typography, logotype, iconography, interaction voice) committed to docs/BRANDING.md with assets and applied to the app shell; no Clauderizer co-branding per proposal 15.2; agents draft candidate directions, the operator chooses
APPROVAL: docs/BRANDING.md — the operator signs off the PhaseKeep brand identity (recorded via cz_approve_gate so later edits read stale)
exit_criteria.detail: approval for 'docs/BRANDING.md' not recorded — record it with cz_approve_gate

exit_criteria.text: Plain-english vocabulary hint layer exists (D-012): popover component plus term content in the clauderizer.com register sourced from the glossary, ready for every view to apply

phases.name: Core Views
phases.goal: Portfolio and gameplan detail, Open Items, Invariants and Decisions - the proposal 8.1 core three - rendered from contract snapshots with campaign vocabulary, supersession chains, and approval staleness.

exit_criteria.text: Portfolio view renders every open gameplan with kind, focus marker, phase progress, baseline, and a pending-cascade badge; campaign-kind gameplans render in their kind vocabulary

Gameplan detail renders phases with exit-criteria state including completed-with-unchecked-criteria surfaced loudly, decisions, amendments, current handoff, and a light phase/task progress board (D-013)
Campaign-kind gameplan detail renders the deliverable lifecycle board (deliverables against the kind's lifecycle statuses)
Open Items view lists all tracked unknowns with stable IDs, filterable by gameplan, age, and resolution state
Invariants view shows scope and audience; Decisions view renders supersession chains as chains; approval states show content-hash staleness
Domain terms in the core views carry their plain-english hints (D-012)
All views are pure renders of contract snapshots with fixture tests

phases.name: Graph, Lessons, and Docs Views
phases.goal: The in-milestone fast-follows: dependency DAG with cascade overlay and cz_consumes edges, Lessons with curation state and memory gauge, Corrections, and rendered Docs with open-in-editor.

exit_criteria.text: Graph view renders entities, depends_on edges with semver pins, cascade results overlaid (what changed, what was flagged, verdicts), and cz_consumes cross-gameplan edges
Lessons view shows curation state (active, consolidated, promoted, obsoleted) and the memory gauge including the current handoff-size estimate (orientation cost, D-017); Corrections log renders
Docs view renders sanitized markdown for canonical documents with an index sidebar and an open-in-$EDITOR affordance that never grows an editor
All new views fixture-tested; malformed-fixture fuzz keeps every view on last-known-good

phases.name: Multi-Project, Reconciliation Strip, and Scaffold Writes
phases.goal: Project switcher and add-project with consent-gated init and engine acquisition; the reconciliation strip with one-op fix buttons; the empty-gameplan scaffold path (the m1 half of D-010); instance records.

exit_criteria.text: Project switcher keeps which-repo-am-I-looking-at unmistakable; multiple real projects driven daily

Add-project flow: import or create, consent screen naming the engine footprint before clauderize init, engine acquisition ladder (PATH, then uvx/pipx spawn, then one screen naming the missing prerequisite), doctor check surfaced
Reconciliation strip surfaces never-closed complete gameplans, phases transitioned with unchecked criteria, and open items lingering on finished work
One-op fix buttons (check criterion, resolve item) execute through the ops registry under its advisory lock - the only writes, per the write-classes invariant

React-to-state generalization (D-011): surfaced conditions carry their shaped reaction - one-op buttons include advisory report generation (invoke cz_cascade on a pending cascade); judgment-shaped reactions render their dispatch button (staged in m1, live with m3)
Empty-gameplan scaffold path works and its criteria-less phases appear honestly in the reconciliation strip
Instance record (port, PID, roots) written under config; second startup covering the same roots detects and offers the existing URL

phases.name: Daily-Driver Hardening
phases.goal: Meet and measure the proposal 8.4 exit criteria in real daily use across multiple projects: freshness targets, malformed-state resilience, and the operator actually abandoning manual file-opening.

exit_criteria.text: Proposal 8.4 met: dashboard runs against real projects daily; agent-made changes visible within 2s same-side / 3s polled - measured and recorded via cz_add_output
Malformed-state fuzz suite passes: no input blanks the view or crashes the server
Vocabulary-hint sweep passes: every domain term across all shipped views carries its plain-english hint (D-012)
Operator attestation recorded in the phase handoff: memory files no longer opened in an editor to answer what-is-pending
Baseline test count recorded; docs/TESTING.md updated with the fixture-and-pure-function discipline

phases.name: PTY Subsystem and Flow Control
phases.goal: Build the PTY subsystem on the authenticated socket: lazy node-pty load with the honest degrade path, binary WS frames on pty:<id> channels, per-channel backpressure with acknowledged watermarks, resize plumbing; scrollback in memory only.

exit_criteria.text: node-pty loads lazily; a broken native build degrades to dashboard-works-terminals-unavailable with the named fix (proposal 15.3) - tested by breaking the build
PTY spawn, resize, and kill work over dedicated binary WS channels tagged by the channel envelope
Per-channel backpressure: server pauses PTY reads when the client's acknowledged watermark falls behind; cat of a huge file neither wedges the socket nor grows server memory unboundedly - tested
Scrollback lives in browser/xterm.js memory only; server never persists PTY bytes (invariant honored in code review)

phases.name: Tabs and Shell Matrix
phases.goal: Terminal tabs with the full proposal 9.1 shell matrix: per-tab shell choice, per-tab cwd defaulting to the active project root, WSL distro enumeration with UTF-16LE decoding, and interop rows both directions.

exit_criteria.text: Tabs with per-tab shell choice per the 9.1 matrix: $SHELL on Linux/macOS; PowerShell, cmd, and per-distro WSL on Windows; powershell.exe/cmd.exe interop when the core runs inside WSL
wsl -l enumeration decodes UTF-16LE correctly - unit-tested against captured raw bytes
Per-tab working directory defaults to the active project root and is overridable
Interactive TUIs (vim, htop) usable in a WSL tab from the Windows-host browser

phases.name: ConPTY Validation
phases.goal: Exercise PowerShell and cmd through ConPTY on the real Windows host early (proposal 9.2): document quirks rather than heroically work around them, treat unsolved correctness issues as upstream bugs.

exit_criteria.text: PowerShell and cmd exercised through ConPTY on the real Windows host early in the phase, not last
Known cosmetic ConPTY quirks documented in docs/ with the upstream-bug policy; no local heroics for unsolved upstream correctness issues
A Windows host shows PowerShell and WSL tabs side by side (proposal 9.4)
Docs state the fallback position explicitly: native Windows tabs fully supported, WSL tabs the recommended primary Windows experience
Proposal 9.4 overall: daily shell work happens in the UI without missing a native terminal; large outputs do not wedge the socket

phases.name: Terminal-First Gameplan Creation
phases.goal: The D-010 flow and designated demo scene: plus-button opens a pty with the planning prompt staged for the assigned agent while the portfolio pane populates live beside it off the revision signal.

exit_criteria.text: Portfolio plus-button (primary path) launches the assigned/manager agent in a pty in the repo with the planning prompt staged, and the creation conversation runs in that terminal - the operator's click is the explicit trigger, the UI never starts a planning session silently, and the operator can take over the pty at any point
Portfolio pane populates live beside the terminal during a real planning conversation, driven by the revision signal - the D-010 demo scene works end to end
Vocabulary chips beside the terminal input stage operative phrases with one-line plain-english hints; chips never execute - the operator sends (D-012, un-parked Appendix B)
Secondary empty-gameplan option still scaffolds bare structure through the ops registry
Live-populate latency during a write burst feels immediate (revision-signal path, not watch), verified against a real planning session

phases.name: Agent Profiles and Discovery
phases.goal: Agents as data: TOML profiles (command, host, cwd policy, env), discovery probing which and versions natively and per WSL distro with operator-confirmed proposals, and an agent list composing discovery presence with clauderize doctor wiring status.

exit_criteria.text: Agent profiles load as TOML data (command, host, working-directory policy, env additions, optional env allowlist); adding an agent is a new file, not a release
(agent, host) identity honored: the same tool on both sides of the WSL boundary is two agents
Discovery probes which natively and per enumerated distro via wsl.exe (plus reverse interop from tab-mode-in-WSL), captures versions, proposes profiles; operator confirms before anything is saved
Agent list shows discovery result and clauderize doctor wiring status side by side (compose, not compete)

phases.name: tmux Substrate Prototype
phases.goal: Prototype tmux control mode (-CC) versus plain attach early because it shapes the session registry (proposal 13.5, 16.6); decide, record the decision, and build the metadata-only registry with restart reattachment.

exit_criteria.text: tmux control mode versus plain attach prototyped both ways; decision recorded as a D-NNN with evidence; open item resolved
Generated session naming scheme distinguishes PhaseKeep-managed sessions
Session registry: metadata-only JSON, atomic writes, under XDG config; never scrollback
Server restart re-binds live tmux sessions by enumeration plus registry match; browser refresh loses nothing - both tested

Instance policy graduates for session management (proposal 7.2): a second instance detects an existing session-managing daemon over the same namespace and defers instead of competing - tested with two concurrent starts; an flock on the journal/registry directory backs the check so a racing second dispatcher fails loudly (D-019)

phases.name: Sessions v1
phases.goal: Agent sessions as supervised PTYs with metadata (proposal 13.2): launch, observe across projects, attach, reattach, terminate, post-exit inspection, gameplan cross-links, and the per-session media output gallery.

exit_criteria.text: Launch a session from a profile into a project with gameplan/phase context attached; sessions view shows all sessions across projects with liveness and age
Any session attaches to a terminal pane; reattach works after browser refresh and server restart; graceful terminate signals then escalates

Checkpoint button on a live session sends the staged memory-sync prompt into the pty on the operator's explicit click (D-016); the dashboard reflects the agent's resulting writes via the revision signal - input only, no output ever read
An exited session's terminal state stays inspectable until dismissed
Gameplans show their linked live sessions; sessions link back to their gameplan

Per-session gallery renders media files landing in the session outputs directory; the directory is a per-profile data convention (declared in the profile, exported to the session, e.g. as an env var the agent may use) - cooperation-optional, degrading to an empty gallery, never an agent-specific code path (INVARIANT-08); no counterfeit interactive MCP widgets (proposal 13.2)

phases.name: Assignments and the Manager Role
phases.goal: Assignments as Clauderizer state rendered and edited through the ops registry (proposal 13.3), plus the manager as a reassignable per-project role dispatched fresh per repo, never a roving resident.

exit_criteria.text: Phase and gameplan assignments render from Clauderizer memory (the m0 assignment field) and edit through the ops registry; no UI-local assignment state
Assignments ship with the repo: visible in diffs, survive the UI, readable by agents
Manager role assignable and reassignable per project; manager dispatches run as fresh per-repo sessions that end - no roving resident session

phases.name: Dispatcher Launch Rung and Reconciliation
phases.goal: The crash-only dispatcher core (proposal 13.4): click-to-launch with staged prompts, every wake recomputing desired state from contract reads plus assignments plus flags, and the reconciliation strip's dispatch-manager button.

exit_criteria.text: Launch rung: one click spawns the assigned agent in the repo with the do-next-phase or manager-hygiene prompt staged

Crash-only: on every wake (server start, browser reattach, timer, session end, change signal) the dispatcher recomputes what should run from contract reads plus assignments plus continuation flags; it holds no authoritative state
Kill the server mid-flight and restart: the dispatcher converges to the same decisions - tested
Reconciliation strip ritual findings gain the dispatch-manager button; checkbox findings keep their one-op buttons

phases.name: Continue Rung, Budgets, and Chain-Breakers
phases.goal: Auto-continue under the absolute completion rule (D-008), per-(phase,agent) attempt counters, time-and-count budgets, the standing-condition dispatch gate, and the append-only JSONL event journal.

exit_criteria.text: Continue fires only when the session process exited AND the phase transitioned per contract reads; exit-without-transition is recorded as a stall (D-008) - integration-tested with a deterministic stall

Kill-complete semantics per D-014: a session terminated by budget/wall-clock whose phase transition is observed counts as a completion, never a stall; the attempt counter does not increment and the journal records the case distinctly

Attempt counter per (phase, agent) stops the chain at max attempts; an attempt is a prompt delivered to a live process and journaled (D-019) - a crash before launch consumes nothing; counters are reconstructed from the journal on wake so a restart cannot reset them
Wall-clock and session-count budgets enforced from pty lifecycle alone; the continue rung dispatches only run-to-completion profiles (D-014) pending the O-04 successor decision
Dispatcher declines dispatch while a standing condition is tripped or the counter is exhausted; manual launch is never blocked

The journal is a write-ahead log, not just history (D-019): intents fsynced with idempotency keys and a boot epoch before action, committed/aborted after; append-only JSONL under XDG; tmux enumeration is ground truth on wake and the journal replays intent

Replay rules tested: uncommitted reap finishes idempotently; uncommitted reuse quarantines (never auto-reaped, never dispatched over, surfaced); uncommitted dispatch with no live session consumes no attempt

Quiescence probes per D-020: process-group I/O/CPU deltas from /proc with the W_idle flat window; dispatch-time baseline snapshot in the registry entry with session dirt computed as the delta; unpushed commits journaled as advisory, never a reap blocker; native Windows documented as time-only quiescence

Shadow mode before spine (D-020): the conjunction runs advisory-first journaling would_reap/would_inject with per-decision evidence objects; enforcement flips only via an explicit O-04 resolution citing the calibration journal
No push notifications exist: chain-breaks, trips, and completions render in the UI only (the UI is the pager; the morning reconciliation pass is the newspaper)

phases.name: Loops and Orphans
phases.goal: The loops rung behind its own opt-in for loop-kind gameplans, orphan session surfacing with adopt/kill, global concurrency caps, and the proposal 13.6 Tier-1 verification pass.

exit_criteria.text: Loops rung crank works for loop-kind gameplans behind its own per-gameplan opt-in, honoring max-sessions-per-day and the global concurrency cap
Orphans (tmux sessions matching the scheme without registry entries, and vice versa) surface with adopt and kill actions; never silently ignored, never counted as managed attempts
Proposal 13.6 verified on Tier 1: server restart loses zero live sessions and zero journal history; no session exists without appearing in the sessions view
Multiple concurrent agents across multiple repos run through the UI daily; README documents the loop-authority sentence

phases.name: Electron Shell and IPC Transport
phases.goal: Wrap the identical server core in an Electron shell speaking IPC with the loopback listener off by default and a single-instance lock; prove the m1 security controls dormant-not-deleted by enabling the listener and passing the full suite.

exit_criteria.text: Electron app boots the identical server core in-process; renderer speaks IPC; loopback listener off by default in app mode and enableable for tab parity
Single-instance lock: second launch focuses the first
Tray presence: the app minimizes to / lives in the system tray, keeping sessions and watchers alive with the window closed
Security controls verified dormant-not-deleted: enabling the listener in app mode passes the full m1 security test suite
Tab-mode npx channel still works from the same codebase

phases.name: Packaging Matrix
phases.goal: electron-builder producing the three-OS installer set with node-pty rebuilt against both ABIs (Electron in-app, Node for the npx channel), both artifacts smoke-tested in CI (proposal 15.3).

exit_criteria.text: electron-builder produces NSIS/MSI, dmg, and AppImage plus deb/rpm artifacts in CI
node-pty ships rebuilt against the Electron ABI in the app and the Node ABI in the npm package; both artifacts built and smoke-tested in CI (proposal 15.3)
npx phasekeep boots from the published package with prebuilt static assets; startup banner prints the running version
engines field plus startup check yields the human-readable Node-version error

phases.name: Signing, Notarization, and Auto-Update
phases.goal: Windows signing identity and Apple notarization wired into CI as recurring obligations accepted with eyes open (proposal 12.4), electron-updater against GitHub Releases, update check disableable.

exit_criteria.text: Windows artifacts signed (Azure Trusted Signing or OV/EV) and macOS notarized, both in CI - the human procurement open item resolved first
Fresh default-config Windows and macOS VMs install without SmartScreen/Gatekeeper unknown-publisher blocks
electron-updater against GitHub Releases updates N-1 to N in a real test; update check disableable; no other outbound calls (telemetry invariant)
Release pipeline documented end to end: tag to signed artifacts to release to auto-update

phases.name: Windows First-Run and WSL Bootstrap
phases.goal: The proposal 12.2 out-of-box flow: detect WSL, enumerate distros, consent-gated uv/uvx plus engine bootstrap per distro, honest native-only degrade, and the stranger test on all three OSes.

exit_criteria.text: First run on Windows detects WSL, enumerates distros decoding UTF-16LE, and per enabled distro offers consent-gated uv/uvx plus engine bootstrap
Machines without WSL run native-only with the persistence limitation stated plainly and a link to (never an execution of) wsl --install
WSL-side repos refresh via revision-signal polling through per-command wsl.exe spawns (proposal 12.2); doctor duties reduced to interop-responds plus engine-runs-per-distro
Interop-created tmux agent sessions inside a distro survive the Windows app closing entirely and are readopted on next launch - tested
Milestone 3.5 stranger test: on each OS, download to rendered dashboard on a real repo without touching a terminal, showing a signed publisher

phases.name: Site and README
phases.goal: Ship the public face: both domains live with the brand, README with tier policy and the authority sentence, license and NOTICE attribution correct, repository public with issue templates and triage labels ready.

exit_criteria.text: phasekeep.com and phasekeep.dev live with the Milestone 1 brand

README carries the tier policy, the blunt loop-mode authority sentence, the zero-telemetry statement, keep-repos-on-ext4 WSL guidance, and the npx @latest convention with the version-banner triage note (15.3)

License confirmed Apache-2.0 (16.9); NOTICE bundle and about screen attribute Clauderizer correctly; concealment boundary per 15.2 honored (engine named at consent and attribution surfaces, backgrounded elsewhere)
Repository public with issue templates and tier-policy labels

phases.name: Demo and Polish
phases.goal: Record the designated split-screen demo (planning conversation populating the portfolio live, then the dashboard with live sessions) and run a first-run quality pass on all three OSes.

exit_criteria.text: The designated split-screen demo recorded: a real planning conversation in the terminal populating the portfolio live, then the dashboard with live agent sessions
First-run quality pass on all three OSes with fixes applied
Site and README embed the demo; screenshots current

phases.name: Launch
phases.goal: Show HN and X thread out (human action), issue templates and tier-policy triage ready, success measured as external issues being feature requests rather than install failures.

exit_criteria.text: Show HN post and X thread published (human action)
First-week triage rhythm running; issues labeled against the tier policy
Success signal tracked per proposal 18: external issues are feature requests rather than install failures; at least one external contract consumer watched for over six months
